title: execute_cron_create UUID-based id is exposed in output but used for delete — bad UX coupling
description:
## Severity
Trivial

## Dimension
Naming / API Design

## Location
src/tools/cron.rs:195-200 (cron_create result) and 203-228 (cron_delete)

## Violation
cron_create returns "Created schedule '{name}' (id: {id})" with an opaque 8-char hex id. cron_delete then accepts EITHER id OR name (via `args.get("id").or_else(|| args.get("name"))` on line 207-209). The model is given two identifiers for one entity, with no guidance on which to use. Worse, the name is user-supplied and human-readable; the id is system-generated and easy to mistype. The "id" exists only because someone thought "names might collide" — but cron_create already enforces unique names (line 170). So the id is YAGNI duplication of the unique name.

## Mandated refactor
Make `name` the sole identifier. Delete the `id` field from Schedule entirely. cron_delete takes only `name`. cron_list displays only the name, the cron expression, and a creation timestamp. (If a stable id is needed for some external integration, expose `created_at` — it's already an RFC3339 timestamp which is unique-enough for human-scale schedule counts.)

## Filed from QA review dispatch
